query: Venezuela crude oil exports to United States May 2026 tanker tracking shipments
answer:
Based on the extensive research gathered from the first agent (which successfully retrieved data from multiple sources), here is my comprehensive synthesis:

---

# Venezuela Crude Oil Exports to the United States — May 2026 Research Report

## Executive Summary

Venezuela's oil export landscape has been fundamentally transformed following the **January 3, 2026 US military intervention** that captured President Nicolás Maduro. A rapid series of OFAC general licenses (GL 46–50) has reopened Venezuelan crude flows to the US. By **April 2026**, Venezuela's total oil exports surged to **1.23 million barrels per day (bbl/d)** — the highest since 2018. US imports of Venezuelan crude recovered from a blockade-era low of **6,000 bbl/d (July 2025)** to **231,000 bbl/d (February 2026)**, with tanker traffic accelerating through March–April.

---

## 1. US Imports of Venezuelan Crude Oil — Monthly Volumes (EIA Data)

| Month | Imports (thousand bbl/d) | Context |
|-----------|--------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| 2024-07 | 308 | Pre-blockade peak (Chevron license) |
| 2025-01 | 287 | Pre-blockade |
| 2025-04 | 170 | Maritime blockade begins |
| 2025-07 | **6** | Near-zero: full US maritime blockade |
| 2025-09 | 102 | Recovery begins |
| 2025-12 | 137 | Intervention (Jan 3) shifts policy |
| **2026-01** | **200** | Post-intervention; GL 46 issued Jan 29 |
| **2026-02** | **231** | Multiple GLs expand authorization |
| 2026-03–05 | *Not yet published by EIA* | Tanker data indicates continued acceleration |

**Source:** U.S. EIA, Series MCRIMUSVE2

---

## 2. Tanker Tracking & Shipment Data (April–May 2026)

- **April 2026**: Venezuela's **total** oil exports reached **1.23 million bbl/d** — a **seven-year high**.
- **66 cargo vessels** departed Venezuelan ports in April 2026, up from **61 vessels in March** (~1.08 million bbl/d).
- This represents a **14% month-over-month increase** from March to April.
- Key destinations: **United States, India, and Europe**.
- **Chevron** is actively importing Venezuelan heavy crude into **US Gulf Coast refineries** (Pascagoula, MS and others).
- **Citgo Petroleum** bought Venezuelan crude for the first time since 2019 in late January 2026.
- The first cargo of **460,000 barrels of naphtha** (diluent) by trading house **Vitol** arrived in Venezuela on January 23, 2026.

**Source:** OilPrice.com (May 1, 2026), Reuters (May 1, 2026), BBC (April 7, 2026)

---

## 3. Sanctions & Licensing Framework (Post-Intervention)

| Date | Action |
|--------------|--------|
| Jan 29, 2026 | **GL 46**: Broad authorization for US entities to purchase/transport Venezuelan oil |
| Feb 3, 2026 | **GL 47**: US-origin diluent exports to Venezuela authorized |
| Feb 10, 2026 | **GL 48**: Goods/services for oil exploration & production authorized |
| Feb 13, 2026 | **GL 49**: Contingent contracts for new investment; **GL 50**: BP, Chevron, Eni, Repsol, Shell broadly authorized |
| Feb 18, 2026 | **GL 50A**: Maurel & Prom added |
| Mar 13, 2026 | Sanctions waivers expanded (fertilizer, electricity) |
| Mar 18, 2026 | US broadly authorizes all PDVSA transactions |

**Key restriction:** Royalties/taxes paid into **US-controlled deposit fund**, not directly to Venezuelan government. No transactions with Iran, Russia, China, North Korea, or Cuba-linked entities.

**Sources:** Hunton Andrews Kurth, Morgan Lewis, OFAC

---

## 4. Venezuela Total Production & Export Capacity

- **Historical peak**: ~3.2 million bbl/d (late 1990s)
- **Pre-sanctions (2018)**: ~1.2–1.4 million bbl/d
- **Post-sanctions low (2020–2023)**: ~600,000–900,000 bbl/d
- **April 2026 exports**: **1.23 million bbl/d** (implying production somewhat higher)
- **Key crude grades**: Merey 16, Hamaca, Boscan, Petrozuata blends (ultra-heavy, require diluent)
- Rebuilding full capacity will require **tens of billions of dollars** over several years.

During the 2026 State of the Union, Trump stated the US had received "more than **80 million barrels of oil**" from Venezuela.

---

## 5. Key Companies & Entities

| Entity | Role |
|--------|------|
| **Chevron** | Primary US importer; Gulf Coast refinery operations |
| **Citgo** | US refiner (formerly PDVSA-owned); resumed Venezuelan crude purchases Jan 2026 |
| **BP, Eni, Repsol, Shell** | Authorized under GL 50 for broad Venezuelan operations |
| **Maurel & Prom** | Added to GL 50A (Feb 18) |
| **ExxonMobil, ConocoPhillips** | Sent assessment teams to Venezuela |
| **Vitol** | Trading house; delivered first diluent cargo |
| **PDVSA** | Venezuelan state oil company; now open to private majority ownership |

---

## 6. Data Gaps & Caveats

- **EIA monthly import data** is only available through **February 2026**. March–May 2026 figures have not yet been published.
- **May 2026 tanker-by-tanker tracking** is not publicly available from free sources; services like **Kpler, Vortexa, and TankerTrackers.com** would have real-time vessel-level data.
- The **14% monthly acceleration** in April suggests May 2026 US imports may be in the range of **250,000–350,000+ bbl/d**, but this is extrapolation from shipping trends, not confirmed data.
- Venezuela's production figures carry uncertainty; OPEC secondary sources and independent consultancies (Rystad, Wood Mackenzie) offer the most reliable estimates.

---

key_facts: Venezuela's total oil exports reached 1.23 million bbl/d in April 2026 — the highest level since 2018, carried by 66 cargo vessels (up from 61 in March).
US imports of Venezuelan crude recovered from a blockade low of 6,000 bbl/d (July 2025) to 231,000 bbl/d (February 2026, latest EIA data).
The January 3, 2026 US military intervention in Venezuela and subsequent OFAC General Licenses (GL 46–50) fundamentally reopened Venezuelan oil flows to the US.
GL 46 (Jan 29, 2026) broadly authorized US entities to purchase, transport, and sell Venezuelan-origin crude oil.
Chevron is actively importing Venezuelan heavy crude into US Gulf Coast refineries; Citgo resumed Venezuelan crude purchases for the first time since 2019 in late January 2026.
BP, Eni, Repsol, Shell, and Maurel & Prom were authorized for broad Venezuelan oil operations under GL 50/50A (Feb 13–18, 2026).
Royalties and federal taxes from Venezuelan oil must be paid into a US-controlled foreign government deposit fund, not directly to the Venezuelan government.
Venezuela's new Hydrocarbons Law (Jan 29, 2026) reversed Chavez-era rules requiring PDVSA majority stakes, allowing foreign companies to control production.
The first diluent cargo (460,000 barrels of naphtha by Vitol) arrived in Venezuela on January 23, 2026, critical for processing ultra-heavy crude.
EIA monthly import data for March–May 2026 is not yet published; May 2026 vessel-level tracking requires commercial services (Kpler, Vortexa, TankerTrackers.com).
During the 2026 State of the Union, Trump stated the US had received more than 80 million barrels of oil from Venezuela.
US Energy Secretary Chris Wright reported oil sales exceeded $1 billion as of February 2026.
